OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY DEFT IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CONEFF, MICHAEL
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY DEFENDANT A & B PAINTING INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT A & B PAINTING INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT HAVE A DISABILITY WHEN HE APPLIED FOR WORK WITH DEFENDANT IN OCTOBER 2010. EVEN IF DEFENDANT'S EVIDENCE WAS SUFFICIENT TO SHIFT THE BURDEN, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANTS DISCRIMINATED ON THE BASIS OF A PERCEIVED DISABILITY AND WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS DISABLED. (SEE PLAINTIFF'S ADDITIONAL MATERIAL FACTS NOS. 116-118 AND 144-145.) ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH HIS DIAGNOSIS THROUGH HIS OWN TESTIMONY (AND DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED TO THAT EXTENT), HIS TESTIMONY IS ADMISSIBLE TO ESTABLISH HIS UNDERSTANDING OF HIS SYMPTOMS AND THE EFFECTS OF HIS CONDITION, SUCH AS HIS NEED TO TAKE BREAKS FOR FRESH AIR (AND THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED TO THAT EXTENT). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-04-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-07-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. P. BOBBY SHUKLA OF THE LAW OFFICES OF STEPHEN M. MURPHY, TEL. NO. 986-1338 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RICHARD A. LEVINE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF JEFFREY F. PACCASSI, TEL. NO. 932-7236 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT A & B PAINTING INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is Denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden to show that Plaintiff did not have a disability when he applied for work with Defendant in October 2010. Even if Defendant's evidence was sufficient to shift the burden, Plaintiff has raised a triable issue regarding whether Defendants discriminated on the basis of a perceived disability and whether Plaintiff was disabled. (See Plaintiff's Additional Material Facts Nos. 116-118 and 144-145.) Although Plaintiff cannot establish his diagnosis through his own testimony (and Defendants' objections are sustained to that extent), his testimony is admissible to establish his understanding of his symptoms and the effects of his condition, such as his need to take breaks for fresh air (and the objections are overruled to that extent). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/PJB)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2013 Continued To Feb-20-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling, which was corrected due to a typographical error, as follows: DEFENDANT A & B PAINTING INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued on the Court's own motion to February 20, 2013 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give A&B Painting the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. A&B Painting must immediately deliver courtesy copies of their motion and reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, or the motion will be denied. The court has courtesy copies of the opposition. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
